label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, so there's this problem about 100 lockers that are all closed at first. Then, there's a bunch of people, from Person 1 to Person 100, each doing some actions on the lockers. Person 1 opens every locker. Person 2 toggles every second locker. Person k toggles every k-th locker, and so on up to k = 100. The question is, how many lockers are open at the end? Hmm, let's try to break this down. First, all lockers start closed. Then, each person toggles certain lockers. Toggling means if it's closed, they open it, and if it's open, they close it. So Person 1 is opening all lockers. Then Person 2 is toggling every 2nd one. So locker 2, 4, 6, etc. Then Person 3 toggles every 3rd locker, like locker 3, 6, 9, and so on. Now, the key is to figure out for each locker how many times it's been toggled. Because if a locker is toggled an even number of times, it will end up closed (since starting from closed, even toggles bring it back to closed). If it's toggled an odd number of times, it will be open. So the problem reduces to finding how many numbers between 1 and 100 have an odd number of divisors.
